summary: An escalating conflict between Iran and the United States dominates global headlines, with Trump claiming the war will end quickly while Iran reviews American peace proposals, as Israel continues bombing targets in Lebanon and the broader Middle East becomes destabilized by soaring oil prices affecting economies worldwide. Military developments are accelerating, including Turkey's unveiling of its first intercontinental ballistic missile and mounting international military deployments toward the Strait of Hormuz, while North Korea signals it rejects nuclear non-proliferation treaties. Beyond the Middle East, China is moving forward with anti-sanctions legislation and has announced suspended death sentences for former defense ministers, Australia is dealing with returning ISIS-linked women, and several nations face domestic political turbulence ranging from Korean court cases to German coalition tensions and UK local elections. Rising fuel costs and geopolitical uncertainty are creating economic stress across Asia, Africa, and the Gulf region, with some corporations like Shell seeing profits rise amid the turmoil.
